channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Name That Toon Contest
Exposed Fuel Tank Gauges Under Attack in the US
item.description: Threat actors are taking advantage of Internet-exposed tank gauges by breaching gas stations, opening the door to disruption.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: IronWorm and New Miasma Worm Variant Hit npm in Supply Chain Attacks
item.description:
Multiple software supply chain attacks have hit the npm ecosystem, with threat actors using both malicious and poisoned versions of over 50 legitimate packages to distribute a Rust-based information stealer and a self-spreading worm, respectively.
According to JFrog, the information stealer "scrapes every secret it can find on a developer's machine, hides behind an eBPF kernel rootkit, and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OWASP Incubator Project Helps Developers Find and Fix Vulnerable Dependencies in Seconds
item.description: <p>CVE Lite CLI is a free, open-source command line tool that scans your projects in seconds and tells you exactly which included packages contain a vulnerability.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/owasp-incubator-project-helps-developers-find-and-fix-vulnerable-dependencies-in-seconds/">OWASP Incubator Project Helps Developers Find and Fix Vulnerable Dependencies in Seconds</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend

item.title: New CISA Warning: Hackers Are Targeting Fuel Tank Monitoring Systems
item.description: <p>CISA warns attackers are targeting internet-exposed Automatic Tank Gauge systems used in fuel storage. Here’s what operators should fix now.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-cisa-atg-systems-fuel-storage-cyberattacks/">New CISA Warning: Hackers Are Targeting Fuel Tank Monitoring Systems</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Android Spyware Asin Targets Arabic Users via Fake News, PDF and War Map Apps
item.description:
Arabic-speaking users have emerged as the target of a new Android spyware codenamed Asin, according to findings from ESET.
The Slovakian cybersecurity company said it first detected the malware spread via multiple campaigns in early 2025, with each attack wave making use of distinct websites mimicking utilities, war-related updates, and a government news source:
govlens[.]net, which
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: DentaQuest Cyberattack Tied to 2.6M Exposed Accounts
item.description: <p>DentaQuest confirmed a cybersecurity incident after health data tied to 2.6 million accounts surfaced in a public breach listing.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-dentaquest-breach-health-data-2-6m-accounts/">DentaQuest Cyberattack Tied to 2.6M Exposed Accounts</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Adaptive, Agentic AI Worms Loom as Next Enterprise Threat
item.description: AI worms, or &quot;viruses with wings and brains,&quot; adapt to new environments, seek out vulnerabilities, and will likely strike within a year, researchers say.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: AI Worm
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: In Other News: Anthropic Maps AI Threats, Unpatched Comodo Flaw, Palantir Chief Eyed for CISA
item.description: <p>Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Ultrahuman data leak, The Gentlemen ransomware analysis, Hola Browser bundles miner.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/in-other-news-anthropic-maps-ai-threats-unpatched-comodo-flaw-palantir-chief-eyed-for-cisa/">In Other News: Anthropic Maps AI Threats, Unpatched Comodo Flaw, Palantir Chief Eyed for CISA</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News

item.title: Trump AI Order Seeks Voluntary Frontier Model Testing
item.description: The White House's executive order establishes voluntary framework for early government access to frontier models while investing in federal security.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: New Threat Cluster OP-512 Targets Microsoft IIS Servers with Custom Web Shell Framework
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously unreported threat cluster dubbed OP-512 (where "OP" stands for "opponent") that has been observed&nbsp;targeting Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS) servers to deploy a bespoke web shell framework.
ReliaQuest has assessed with moderate to high confidence that the espionage-focused activity is linked to China.
"OP-512 was highly
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Southeast Asia Scam Compounds Turn AI Into a Cybersecurity Threat
item.description: <p>Scam compounds across Southeast Asia are using AI, malware, and automation to scale fraud, forcing APAC security teams to rethink phishing, identity, and mobile-risk controls.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-ai-scam-compounds-risk-apac-southeast-asia/">Southeast Asia Scam Compounds Turn AI Into a Cybersecurity Threat</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Hackers Leak DentaQuest Information Impacting 2.6 Million
item.description: <p>The ShinyHunters extortion group leaked roughly 234 GB of data allegedly stolen from the dental benefits administrator.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/hackers-leak-dentaquest-information-impacting-2-6-million/">Hackers Leak DentaQuest Information Impacting 2.6 Million</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Only 10% of SOCs Say They’re Getting Excellent Value From AI. Here’s What the Second Wave Has to Deliver
item.description: Eighteen months ago, the AI SOC was a marketing line. Today it's a budget item. The category has crossed over from interesting to inevitable, with billions of dollars now flowing into AI-powered security operations platforms, agentic SOC tools, and AI co-pilots built into every layer of the security stack. The data shows SOCs are buying, deploying, and standing up AI capabilities at the fastest
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Chrome 149 Patches 429 Vulnerabilities
item.description: <p>Over 100 bugs are critical or high-severity, mainly use-after-free and insufficient validation of untrusted input flaws.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/chrome-149-patches-429-vulnerabilities/">Chrome 149 Patches 429 Vulnerabilities</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Industry Reactions to New Trump AI Cybersecurity Executive Order: Feedback Friday
item.description: <p>Experts commented on the EO’s voluntary nature, the balance between innovation and security, and potential implementation gaps.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/industry-reactions-to-new-trump-ai-cybersecurity-executive-order-feedback-friday/">Industry Reactions to New Trump AI Cybersecurity Executive Order: Feedback Friday</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News

item.title: Five Eyes: Chinese Spies Target Government, Military Staff With Fake Job Opportunities
item.description: <p>Posing as recruiters on online platforms, Chinese intelligence officers target personnel with access to classified or privileged information.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/five-eyes-chinese-spies-target-government-military-staff-with-fake-job-opportunities/">Five Eyes: Chinese Spies Target Government, Military Staff With Fake Job Opportunities</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Hackers Exploit Critical Everest Forms Pro WordPress Plugin Flaw to Take Over Sites
item.description:
Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical security flaw in Everest Forms Pro, a WordPress plugin with about 4,000 active installations, to execute arbitrary code, leading to a complete site compromise.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-3300 (CVSS score: 9.8), a remote code execution bug impacting all versions of the plugin up to, and including, 1.9.12. A patch for the flaw was
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Nightclub Giant RCI Says Data Breach Affects 40,000 Individuals
item.description: <p>The company detected a network intrusion in March and an investigation showed that some files were stolen during the attack.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/nightclub-giant-rci-says-data-breach-affects-40000-individuals/">Nightclub Giant RCI Says Data Breach Affects 40,000 Individuals</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Scams Are Already Live: Fake Sites, Banking Malware, and Stolen Logins
item.description:
Security researchers and the FBI are warning that a wave of FIFA-themed fraud is already hitting World Cup 2026 fans, days before the June 11 kickoff.
Recent reports describe thousands of lookalike FIFA domains, banking malware hidden inside pirate streaming apps, and at least one operation that copies FIFA's login page well enough to take over real accounts.
It is an obvious target. More than
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cisco Warns of 7th SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in 2026
item.description: <p>The vulnerability is tracked as CVE-2026-20245 and it can allow arbitrary command execution as root, but no patch yet.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cisco-warns-of-7th-sd-wan-zero-day-exploited-in-2026/">Cisco Warns of 7th SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in 2026</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: PCPJack Hijacks 230 AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure Servers for Covert SMTP Relay Network
item.description:
The threat actor known as PCPJack has hijacked cloud servers associated with Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure to create a covert SMTP email relay network.
"Compromised business servers across the U.S., Europe, and Asia were quietly converted into SMTP proxies, verified for mail relay capability, and synced to a downstream consumer every five minutes," Hunt.io said in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Top Vendor Risk Monitoring Solutions for Continuous Oversight | UpGuard
item.description: Explore the best vendor risk monitoring solutions that provide continuous oversight of third-party vendors, ensuring compliance and security.

item.title: Rust-Written IronWorm Hits NPM Supply Chain
item.description: Like Shai-Hulud, the campaign targets developers to steal credentials and reuses them to propagate across the software supply channel.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
item.description: One of the world's most diverse, least-focused cybercrime groups is enlarging its footprint beyond East Asia.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: 4 Critical Threats Where Attackers Have the Advantage
item.description: Gartner analysts issued a call to action to bolster defenses against several emerging critical threats, such as deepfakes and prompt injections.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Apple Begins Rosetta’s Final Phase as Intel Mac Era Winds Down
item.description: <p>Apple says macOS 26 Tahoe is the last major release for Intel Macs, with Rosetta support continuing through macOS 27 before narrowing.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-macos-27-drops-intel-mac-support-rosetta/">Apple Begins Rosetta&#8217;s Final Phase as Intel Mac Era Winds Down</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: New GitHub Zero-Day Exposed Developer Tokens to Attackers
item.description: <p>A github.dev flaw could let attackers steal GitHub OAuth tokens through a one-click attack, exposing private repositories and codebases.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-github-dev-vscode-webview-oauth-token-vulnerability/">New GitHub Zero-Day Exposed Developer Tokens to Attackers</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Gartner SRM 2026 Signals a Cybersecurity Shift From Prevention to Resilience
item.description: <p>Gartner SRM 2026 put resilience, identity, and AI agent governance at the center of cybersecurity strategy as prevention loses ground.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-gartner-srm-2026-resilience-ai-security/">Gartner SRM 2026 Signals a Cybersecurity Shift From Prevention to Resilience</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tim Freestone

item.title: Cisco Patches CVE-2026-20230 in Unified CM as Exploit Code Goes Public
item.description:
Cisco has patched a bug in Unified Communications Manager that lets an unauthenticated attacker on the network write files to the box and, from there, climb to root.
It is tracked as CVE-2026-20230, and proof-of-concept exploit code is already public. Cisco's PSIRT says it has not seen the flaw used in attacks yet. The PoC shortens that runway.
The flaw is a server-side request forgery.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Reportedly Wants Android App Code From Play Store Developers
item.description: <p>Google is reportedly offering select Play Store developers payment for access to app source code. For Android developers in APAC, the offer raises questions about IP rights, security risks, AI-use terms, and local data obligations before signing.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apac-google-android-code-offer/">Google Reportedly Wants Android App Code From Play Store Developers</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Microsoft 365 Android Apps Had a Token Flaw IT Teams Should Check Now
item.description: <p>A debug flag left active in six Microsoft 365 Android apps allowed another installed app on the same device to request account tokens without user interaction.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-365-android-token-flaw/">Microsoft 365 Android Apps Had a Token Flaw IT Teams Should Check Now</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: US Firms Try DeepSeek as Silicon Valley AI Costs Rise
item.description: <p>US firms are testing China’s DeepSeek as Silicon Valley AI costs rise, raising questions about savings, data residency, and risk.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-us-firms-try-deepseek-ai-costs-rise/">US Firms Try DeepSeek as Silicon Valley AI Costs Rise</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Claude Code GitHub Action Flaw Let One Malicious Issue Hijack Repositories
item.description:
A security researcher found a flaw in Anthropic's Claude Code GitHub Action that let an attacker take over vulnerable public repositories running it, with nothing more than a single opened GitHub issue. Because Anthropic's own action repo used the same workflow, a working attack could have pushed malicious code into the action itself and onto the projects downstream that pull it.
RyotaK of GMO
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Agentic AI Is Transforming Defense, But Only Secure IT Infrastructure Will Maximize It
item.description: Over the past several weeks, the cybersecurity community has been reminded how quickly frontier and agentic AI in defense networks can challenge our assumptions. When Anthropic's Claude Mythos model was made available to a limited set of organizations as a technical preview, it was reported that an unauthorized group claimed that it had gained access within hours. The incident, if true, was
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Offroad Emerges From Stealth With $7 Million to Tackle Enterprise Identity Risk
item.description: <p>As AI agents, machine identities, and third-party applications multiply across enterprises, Offroad is betting autonomous security agents can restore control over an increasingly unmanageable identity landscape.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/offroad-emerges-from-stealth-with-7-million-to-tackle-enterprise-identity-risk/">Offroad Emerges From Stealth With $7 Million to Tackle Enterprise Identity Risk</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend

item.title: Webinar Today: Third-Party Risk in Practice – Where Programs Break Down and How to Respond
item.description: <p>Join this live webinar as we examine the gap between how organizations think their third-party risk programs are performing and what’s actually happening in practice. </p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/webinar-today-third-party-risk-in-practice-where-programs-break-down-and-to-respond/">Webinar Today: Third-Party Risk in Practice &#8211; Where Programs Break Down and How to Respond</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News

item.title: Bugcrowd Launches EU Data Residency Option For Evolving Data Sovereignty Needs
item.description: Organizations are growing serious about what nation’s rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: Malicious WhatsApp, Slack Alerts Could Have Exposed Millions of Android Users
item.description: <p>SafeBreach found a now-fixed Gemini Android flaw that let malicious WhatsApp and Slack alerts manipulate AI responses and tools.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-whatsapp-slack-alerts-could-manipulate-gemini-android/">Malicious WhatsApp, Slack Alerts Could Have Exposed Millions of Android Users</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: AI Agents Gone Wrong, Sketchy C2 Tools, ClickFix Tricks, JS Backdoors & 20+ New Stories
item.description:
It got stupid again.
The internet still feels held together with tape. Bad plugins, old bugs, fake tools, trusted apps doing shady things. Same mess, new wrapper. And now the weird stuff is normal. Forums go down and come back worse. Cheap hackers get better toys. AI starts breaking real systems. Great.
Read the whole thing before it ruins your week anyway.
Unauthenticated
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: China-Linked TA4922 Expands Phishing Attacks to U.K., Germany, Italy, and South Africa
item.description:
A new China-linked cybercrime group known as TA4922 has expanded its targeting focus to target European organizations in the U.K., Germany, Italy, and South Africa.
These efforts have been complemented by a "rapid operational tempo" and a continually evolving malware arsenal comprising known families like ValleyRAT (aka Winos 4.0) and Atlas RAT (aka AtlasCross RAT), as well as previously
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: FlutterShell Backdoor Spreads to macOS via Malicious Google and YouTube Ads
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a macOS malvertising campaign codenamed Operation FlutterBridge that spreads a new backdoor called FlutterShell.
According to Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, the campaign is said to be the next stage of a previously reported activity cluster dubbed JSCoreRunner (aka FileRipple) in late August 2025. The cybercrime group behind the two attack chains is
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hacking Meta’s AI Chatbot
item.description: <p>Hackers are <a href="https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/01/hackers-hijacked-instagram-accounts-by-tricking-meta-ai-support-chatbot-into-granting-access/">convincing</a> Meta&#8217;s AI support chatbot to let them take over other peoples&#8217; accounts:</p> <blockquote><p>A <a href="https://x.com/DarkWebInformer/status/2061253599758315527">video</a> posted on X showed the step-by-step process to hack someone&#8217;s Instagram account. The hacker allegedly used a VPN to spoof the targets&#8217; presumed location to avoid triggering Instagram&#8217;s automated account protections. Then, the hacker opened a chat with Meta AI Support Assistant and asked the bot to add a new email address to the target&#8217;s account. The chatbot can be seen sending a verification code to the email address provided by the hacker; the hacker then shares the verification code with the chatbot, which prompts the chatbot to show a button to &#8220;Reset Password.&#8221; The hacker enters a new password and takes over the victim&#8217;s account...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Fake Sites Mimicking Open-Source Tools Rank High on Google to Deliver Malware via TDS
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a large-scale operation that impersonates open-source and freeware projects to funnel unsuspecting users through a Traffic Distribution System (TDS) and deliver malware families like Remus Stealer, AnimateClipper, and the SessionGate framework.
"The sites are well-designed and often look like legitimate project portals at a glance, sometimes referencing
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hackers Spied on a Stock Exchange Executive's Outlook Mailbox for Five Months
item.description:
Unknown attackers spent at least five months inside the Outlook mailbox of a senior executive at a major global stock exchange, copying the inbox out in small, repeated batches and routing it through Dropbox and OneDrive so the traffic blended into normal cloud activity.
Symantec and Carbon Black's Threat Hunter Team reported the campaign this week. This points to espionage, not a money grab:
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: DoJ Disrupts Southeast Asia Crypto Fraud Networks, Freezes $3.8 Million in Assets
item.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Wednesday announced the results of a sweeping action undertaken by government authorities and private sector companies to combat cyber-enabled and cryptocurrency fraud targeting Americans.
The "Disruption Week" operation began May 18, 2026, leading to the takedown of millions of social media, email, and internet access accounts used by transnational
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Pakistan Spies on Afghan Finance Ministry With Xeno RAT
item.description: Despite broadly connected digital infrastructure, standard fare TTPs are enough to cause trouble for Afghanistan's porous cybersecurity.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
item.description: Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Apple’s 2026 Security Events: iPhone Exploits, Zero-Days Put Millions at Risk
item.description: <p>Apple’s 2026 security year includes zero-days, iPhone exploit kits, WebKit fixes, and background patches that users and IT teams need to track.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-security-roundup-june-2026/">Apple’s 2026 Security Events: iPhone Exploits, Zero-Days Put Millions at Risk</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Tropical Blend: Cyber &amp; Politics Ramp Up Across Latin America
item.description: China-linked espionage groups have attacked at least a dozen nations in the region, gathering information on maritime shipping, oil production, and other geopolitical interests.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: WhatsApp, Slack Notifications Could Hijack Google Gemini on Android
item.description:
A single poisoned notification from WhatsApp, Slack, SMS, Signal, Instagram, or Messenger could have hijacked Google Gemini's voice assistant on Android and made it open a victim's connected windows, fake a message from their boss, push the phone into a Zoom call, or quietly poison its long-term memory.
No malicious app on the phone is required. The assistant just had to treat a hostile
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cyber Insurance Rates Are Dropping, but Exclusions Widen
item.description: Cyber insurance coverage is slowly changing, and some policies may not provide coverage for social engineering attacks like ClickFix.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
item.description: A disabled security setting meant to protect authentication across Android versions of key apps like Word, PowerPoint, and Excel paved the way for attackers to steal logins and data.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: One-Click GitHub Dev Attack Lets Attackers Steal Full GitHub OAuth Tokens
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a one-click attack via Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) that makes it possible to steal a user's GitHub token.
"Just by clicking a link, it's possible for an attacker to steal a GitHub token that can read and write to your repos, including private ones," security researcher Ammar Askar said.
GitHub supports a feature called GitHub.dev that runs as
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Autonomous AI Tool Finds 2-Year-Old RCE Flaw in Redis (CVE-2026-23479)
item.description: Redis has patched a use-after-free in its blocking-client code that lets an authenticated user run arbitrary OS commands on the machine hosting the database. The flaw was found by an autonomous AI tool built to hunt bugs in large codebases. Tracked as CVE-2026-23479, the flaw was introduced in Redis 7.2.0 and remained in every stable branch until the May 5 fixes, unnoticed for over two years.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds Exploited Magento RCE Flaw CVE-2026-45247 to KEV Catalog
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a critical flaw impacting Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento full-page cache extension, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active exploitation in the wild.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-45247 (CVSS score: 9.8), is a case of deserialization of untrusted
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google DoubleClick Abused in New Malspam Campaign to Deliver DesckVB RAT
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malspam campaign that makes use of Google's DoubleClick domain as a way to evade detection and ultimately deliver a remote access trojan (RAT) named DesckVB RAT.
"Before the victim ever reaches attacker-controlled infrastructure, the lure routes through DoubleClick, a legitimate Google-owned domain that many security tools are less likely to treat as
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Flags 2-Year-Old Oracle WebLogic Vulnerability as Actively Exploited
item.description: <p>CISA added Oracle WebLogic flaw CVE-2024-21182 to its KEV catalog, giving federal agencies until June 4 to patch exposed servers.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-cisa-oracle-weblogic-vulnerability-exploited/">CISA Flags 2-Year-Old Oracle WebLogic Vulnerability as Actively Exploited</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Beyond the Zero-Day: See Your Network Like an Attacker | Webinar with HD Moore
item.description:
Assume the breach. Zero-days keep shipping, AI is writing exploits faster than anyone patches, and "patch everything in time" stopped working years ago. Stop betting the org on winning that race. You don't control which bug lands. You control what it can reach once it does.
That is a question about the shape of your network, and most teams have the shape wrong. HD Moore, creator of Metasploit
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft 365 Android Apps Let Any App Steal Account Tokens via Leftover Debug Flag
item.description:
A development flag left switched on in production builds of several Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled the check that limits account-token sharing to trusted Microsoft apps.
Any other app on the same phone could ask for the signed-in user's token and get it, then read email, open files, browse the calendar, and send messages as that user. No password, no login screen, no permission prompt.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Request for Comments: PCI Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) v4.0.1
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Malicious Notifications Could Trick Google Gemini Users
item.description: A prompt injection flaw in Google Gemini's voice assistant let attackers hide malicious commands in notifications, enabling social engineering and more.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Shrinking the IAM Attack Surface through Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP)
item.description:
The Fragmented State of Modern Enterprise Identity
Enterprise IAM is approaching a breaking point. As organizations scale, identity becomes increasingly fragmented across thousands of applications, decentralized teams, machine identities, and autonomous systems.
The result is Identity Dark Matter: identity activity that sits outside the visibility of centralized IAM and beyond the reach of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Used to Decrypt Medieval Ciphers
item.description: <p>Researchers are using machine learning algorithms to <a href="https://www.bbc.com/future/article/20260527-plots-love-letters-and-diplomacy-the-medieval-secrets-being-revealed-by-ai">decrypt</a> historical pencil-and-paper ciphers.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Unpatched Windows Search URI Vulnerability Lets Attackers Steal NTLMv2 Hashes
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an unpatched issue that could be exploited to disclose a user's NTLMv2 hash to the attacker.
Like in the case of CVE-2026-33829, which impacted the Windows Snipping Tool's ms-screensketch: URI handler, the newly flagged issue resides in the search: URI handler, per Huntress.
CVE-2026-33829 refers to a spoofing vulnerability that could expose
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Global Stock Exchange Hit by Monthslong Email Campaign
item.description: A threat actor got a near-continuous view into an influential finance executive's email inbox, thanks to clever use of legitimate, native Windows tools.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Lessons for life: Why children’s data is a long-term identity risk
item.description: Your child’s first data breach may happen before they’ve even opened a bank account. Here’s how to keep their digital life safe.

item.title: New HTTP/2 Bomb Vulnerability Allows Remote DoS on NGINX, Apache, IIS, Envoy & Cloudflare
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a remote denial-of-service exploit that affects major web servers, including NGINX, Apache HTTPD, Microsoft IIS, Envoy, and Cloudflare Pingora.
The vulnerability has been codenamed HTTP/2 Bomb by Calif.
"The vulnerable behavior exists in each server's default HTTP/2 configuration," the company said, adding it was discovered by OpenAI Codex by chaining
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Weedhack Attacks Minecraft Users, CountLoader Hits 86K, Miners Spread via Pirated Content
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new campaign targeting Minecraft players via YouTube to spread malware capable of gaining control of victims' systems.
The Minecraft-focused malware-as-a-service (MaaS) campaign has been codenamed Weedhack by McAfee Labs, stating the activity has been active since January 2026 and impersonates Minecraft clients and mods to infect users. In all, 3820
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Zoom CISO: AI as a Security Enabler, Not Role-Replacer
item.description: Zoom CISO Sandra McLeod discusses the challenges of securing a global communication platform, the promise of AI-driven security workflows, and her advice for aspiring cybersecurity leaders.
item.dc:creator: Kristina Beek

item.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
item.description: Once targeting just Microsoft 365, the phishing-as-a-service platform now aims at AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms, while relying on device code phishing.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: DriveSurge Hijacks Thousands of Sites for ClickFix, FakeUpdate Attacks
item.description: A sneaky, wide-scale IAB operation uses a malicious traffic distribution system (TDS) to redirect visitors of trusted websites to ones that deliver malware.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: China Uses Dual-Method Cyberattack on Czech Orgs
item.description: China is stealing data from high-value targets via a sneaky, double-layer spear-phishing campaign that includes the Azureveil malware.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Cisco Live 2026: New Security Tools Target AI Threats
item.description: <p>Cisco unveiled Cloud Control, Live Protect, and Hybrid Mesh Firewall at Cisco Live to help enterprises manage AI-era IT and security operations.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-cisco-cloud-control-agentic-ai-security/">Cisco Live 2026: New Security Tools Target AI Threats</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Drew Robb

item.title: Securing AI Agents Before They Go Rogue Is Next to Impossible
item.description: High-autonomy agents with broad permissions and unfettered access are a recipe for disaster, and enterprises need to act now before they become the next horror story.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Google June 2026 Android Update Patches 124 Flaws, One Actively Exploited
item.description:
Google on Monday released patches for 124 security vulnerabilities impacting its Android operating system for the month of June 2026, including one high-severity flaw in the Framework component that has come under active exploitation.
Tracked as CVE-2025-48595 (CVSS score: 8.4), the security flaw has been described as a case of privilege escalation without requiring any user interaction. The
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Gamaredon Exploits WinRAR to Deliver GammaWorm and GammaSteel Against Ukraine
item.description:
The Russian hacking group known as Gamaredon has been attributed to the continued exploitation of a WinRAR vulnerability to deliver multiple malware families aimed at data theft and propagation.
Per Sekoia, the activity involves the weaponization of CVE-2025-8088, a path traversal flaw in WinRAR, to launch an HTML Application payload dubbed GammaPhish, which is then used to retrieve an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Oracle WebLogic CVE-2024-21182 Added to KEV Catalog After Active Exploitation
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added a high-severity security flaw impacting Oracle WebLogic Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation.
The vulnerability, CVE-2024-21182 (CVSS score: 7.5), allows an unauthenticated attacker with network access to take control of susceptible servers. It was
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fake Claude Code Installers Deliver Credential-Stealing Malware
item.description: <p>Fake Claude Code install sites are pushing malware that steals API keys, developer credentials, crypto wallets, and other sensitive data.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fake-claude-code-install-sites-malware/">Fake Claude Code Installers Deliver Credential-Stealing Malware</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Google Patches Android Zero-Day Vulnerability in June 2026 Security Update
item.description: <p>Google’s June 2026 Android update fixes dozens of flaws, including a potentially exploited Framework vulnerability and critical system bugs.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-june-2026-android-security-update/">Google Patches Android Zero-Day Vulnerability in June 2026 Security Update</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring In-Solutions Global Ltd
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: FBI Warning: World Cup Scammers Are Spoofing FIFA Tickets, Job Sites
item.description: <p>The FBI warns that fake FIFA websites are targeting World Cup fans with phishing, ticket scams, fake merchandise, and job-related fraud.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fake-fifa-world-cup-sites-fbi-warning/">FBI Warning: World Cup Scammers Are Spoofing FIFA Tickets, Job Sites</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Update Now: Apple Rolls Out Critical Fixes for iPhone 17, M5 Macs
item.description: <p>Apple released iOS 26.5.1 and macOS 26.5.1 to fix iPhone 17 charging issues and M5 Mac shutdown problems before WWDC.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-ios-26-5-1-macos-26-5-1-bug-fixes/">Update Now: Apple Rolls Out Critical Fixes for iPhone 17, M5 Macs</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Beyond Assume-Breach: How AI-Native Security Will Reshape Enterprise Defense
item.description: Twenty years after Dark Reading launched, we're looking ahead at what's next for enterprise security. Spoiler: It's hyper-segmented, AI-orchestrated, and way more sophisticated than your dad's firewall.
item.dc:creator: Fahmida Y. Rashid, Tara Seals

item.title: AI-Driven Exploitation is Destroying Vulnerability Management. Here’s How to Handle It.
item.description:
AI-driven exploitation timelines are rapidly shrinking, and they are not going to stop shrinking. Vulnerabilities are being discovered, reproduced, and weaponized faster than ever in the history of enterprise security. As a result, the window between a vulnerability being disclosed and indiscriminate exploitation observed across the internet is now measured in hours, not days.
The industry's
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Intersection of Encryption and AI
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Microsoft Threatening Security Researcher
item.description: <p>An anonymous security researcher called &#8220;Nightmare Eclipse&#8221; has been <a href="https://deadeclipse666.blogspot.com/">publishing</a> a series of significant security exploits against Microsoft Windows&#8212;including one that <a href="https://arstechnica.com/security/2026/05/zero-day-exploit-completely-defeats-default-windows-11-bitlocker-protections/">breaks</a> BitLocker. Microsoft has <a href="https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/msrc/blog/2026/05/a-shared-responsibility-protecting-customers-through-coordinated-vulnerability-disclosure">threatened</a> legal action against the researcher. Lots of recriminations are being <a href="https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/29/microsoft-under-fire-for-threatening-security-researcher-with-criminal-investigation/">traded</a> back and forth.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: How Leading Organizations Are Turning EDR Into Operational Resilience
item.description:
Most organizations now recognize that endpoint protection alone is no longer sufficient.
That's why adoption of endpoint detection and response (EDR) has accelerated rapidly in recent years. Organizations understand that modern attacks move faster, evade traditional prevention controls, and require continuous visibility into suspicious activity across the environment.
But owning EDR
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Pakistan-Linked SideCopy Targets Afghanistan Finance Ministry with Xeno RAT
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a spear-phishing campaign likely undertaken by the Pakistan-aligned SideCopy group targeting Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance with an open-source remote access trojan called Xeno RAT.
"The campaign opens with a spear phishing delivery - a ZIP archive containing a malicious LNK file bearing a carefully crafted Pashto-language filename,"
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Strava Tightens API Access as AI Scraping Concerns Grow Ahead of IPO
item.description: <p>Strava’s latest API and access changes add new subscription, compliance, and data-use questions for developers building apps on top of the fitness platform.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-strava-api-scraping-crackdown/">Strava Tightens API Access as AI Scraping Concerns Grow Ahead of IPO</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Dashlane Discloses Brute-Force Attack, Encrypted Vaults of Fewer Than 20 Users Downloaded
item.description:
Password manager Dashlane has disclosed that "fewer than" 20 users on the personal subscription plan had their encrypted vaults downloaded following a brute-force attack launched by an unknown party.
On May 31, 2026, the company said an "external" threat actor launched a brute-force attack against certain Dashlane user accounts with the aim of breaking two-factor authentication (2FA)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Surviving a LockBit Ransomware Attack: The ROI of Visibility | UpGuard
item.description: Read how veteran CISO Nick Gicinto saved his alma mater from a LockBit ransomware attack by discarding the standard playbook.

item.title: 12 Best Third-Party Risk Management Software Solutions (2026) | UpGuard
item.description: Learn which contenders are the top TPRM solution options in the market. Compare their features to make the best decision based on your requirements.

item.title: Anthropic to Open Mythos AI to EU's ENISA
item.description: The European security agency's entry to Project Glasswing is the result of &quot;strong bilateral cooperation&quot; between the European Commission and Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
item.description: After a disgruntled security researcher published several zero-day exploits in recent weeks, Microsoft seemingly indicated criminal charges were in order.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
item.description:
A new Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack campaign, codenamed Miasma, has compromised @redhat-cloud-services packages to steal credentials and secrets from developer machines and deliver a self-propagating worm.
"This is effectively a Mini Shai-Hulud campaign: it uses the same core tactics of install-time execution, credential harvesting, CI/CD targeting, encrypted exfiltration, and potential
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Vulnerability Disclosure in the Age of AI
item.description: <p>New article: &#8220;<a href="https://cyberdefensereview.army.mil/Portals/6/Documents/2026-vol11-iss2/CDR_V11_N2_Hathaway.pdf">Responsible Disclosure in the Age of AI: A Call for Urgent Action</a>,&#8221; by Melissa Hathaway.</p> <blockquote><p><b>Abstract:</b> Artificial intelligence is fundamentally reshaping the balance between vulnerability discovery and remediation. Frontier AI models are now capable of autonomously identifying exploitable software vulnerabilities at unprecedented speed and scale. This development exposes decades of accumulated technical debt created by a software industry that prioritized rapid deployment over secure-by-design engineering practices. Drawing on the evolution of software assurance, vulnerability disclosure frameworks, and U.S. cyber policy, this perspective argues that the current moment represents a strategic inflection point for governments, industry, and critical infrastructure operators. The author examines the growing tension between offensive and defensive equities in cyberspace, the emergence of AI-enabled vulnerability discovery capabilities in both the U.S. and China, and the increasing risks posed by unsupported legacy systems and AI-assisted code generation practices. Responsible disclosure can no longer remain a reactive or fragmented process, but must become a coordinated national and international resilience effort involving governments, software vendors, infrastructure operators, and emergency response organizations. The article concludes with an urgent call for accelerated remediation, large-scale patch management coordination, and sustained investment in automated vulnerability repair capabilities before adversaries exploit this rapidly narrowing window of opportunity...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Google Chrome’s New Feature Takes Aim at Cookie Theft, Account Hijacking
item.description: <p>Chrome’s DBSC update binds login sessions to user devices, making stolen session cookies harder to reuse in account hijacking attacks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-chrome-dbsc-session-cookie-theft/">Google Chrome’s New Feature Takes Aim at Cookie Theft, Account Hijacking</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro
